subject: math
source_title: New General Mathematics SSS 1 by M.F. Macrae et al
learning_objectives: Define sine, cosine and tangent of acute angles in a right-angled triangle
Apply SOH CAH TOA to find unknown sides and angles
Use trigonometric tables to find values of trigonometric ratios
Determine lengths of chords using trigonometric ratios and Pythagoras theorem
Draw and interpret graphs of sine and cosine for angles 0° to 360°

sections.heading: Sine, Cosine and Tangent of Acute Angles
sections.content: In a right-angled triangle ABC (right angle at A), with hypotenuse a and sides b and c opposite angles B and C respectively: sin B = b/a (Opposite/Hypotenuse); cos B = c/a (Adjacent/Hypotenuse); tan B = b/c (Opposite/Adjacent). The mnemonic SOH CAH TOA helps recall these. Complementary angles: sin θ = cos(90° − θ) and cos θ = sin(90° − θ).

sections.heading: Using Trigonometric Ratios to Solve Problems
sections.content: Example 1: a triangle has legs 8cm and 5cm with a right angle between them. tan θ = 8/5 = 1.6, so θ = tan⁻¹(1.6) = 58°. The third angle = 90° − 58° = 32°. Example 2: a town Y is 200km from X in direction 40°. Distance Y east of X = 200 × sin 40° = 128.6km.

sections.heading: Determination of Chord Lengths
sections.content: Trigonometric ratios and Pythagoras theorem can find chord lengths. For a chord AB in a circle of radius r, if the perpendicular from the centre O to the chord meets it at B, then AB² + d² = r² (where d is the perpendicular distance). Thus AB = √(r² − d²) and the full chord length = 2 × AB. Also, if the semi-vertical angle θ is known: chord AB = 2r sin θ.

sections.heading: Graphs of Sine and Cosine
sections.content: Using a unit circle (radius = 1), sin θ = y-coordinate and cos θ = x-coordinate of a point on the circle. Both sine and cosine values lie between −1 and +1. Key values: sin 0° = 0, sin 90° = 1, sin 180° = 0, sin 270° = −1, sin 360° = 0; cos 0° = 1, cos 90° = 0, cos 180° = −1, cos 270° = 0, cos 360° = 1. The curves repeat every 360° (they are periodic). The sine and cosine curves have the same shape but different starting points.
